mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 315, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and push the 10-year Treasury yield upward as markets price in prolonged tightness. The mechanism operates continuously when both conditions hold.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 4.33% near threshold, CPIAUCSL at 319.615 elevated
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 55% of 20-day windows when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. Markets interpret this as requiring Fed rate cuts, which compresses long-term Treasury yields downward as growth expectations fall and safe-haven demand increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.2%, near historical lows
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of triggered 20-day windows despite UNRATE increases, or correlation between UNRATE changes and yield direction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1400 thousand units, indicating weakening aggregate demand. This dual demand shock forces the Fed to cut rates, pushing 10-year Treasury yields lower as growth expectations compress and term premium contracts.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 722708M, HOUST at 1501 units
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of 60-day windows when both demand indicators weaken, or Spearman rank correlation between composite demand signal and yield direction is < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation. Markets respond by demanding higher term premium on long-duration assets, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as inflation expectations remain sticky despite growth weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.2062, recent trend required for trigger
regime_hint: stagflation risk or supply shock environment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 55% of 20-day windows following consecutive INDPRO declines, or yield changes show negative correlation with INDPRO momentum (correlation < -0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand per month while UNRATE remains below 4.5%, creating a soft-landing signal. Markets interpret this as the Fed achieving its dual mandate without recession, supporting stable long-term yields as inflation expectations stabilize and growth remains positive.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159398 thousand, UNRATE at 4.2%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 60% of 5-day windows when soft-landing conditions are met, or yield volatility increases rather than stabilizes during these periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized while inflation remains above 3%, creating an overheating scenario. The Fed maintains or raises rates to combat inflation, pushing 10-year Treasury yields upward as markets price in sustained monetary restriction and higher real rates.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in more than 50% of 60-day windows when overheating conditions are present, or real yields (nominal yield minus inflation expectations) do not increase.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains unchanged at current levels (4.33%) for three consecutive months while CPIAUCSL decelerates below 318, signaling the Fed has paused tightening and inflation is cooling. Markets interpret this as the peak of the tightening cycle, reducing term premium and pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 3 months AND CPIAUCSL declines to < 318
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 20-day windows when tightening pause and disinflation signals are present, or term premium does not compress as expected.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 105 while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 150 thousand per month, signaling robust supply-side expansion and labor productivity gains. This productivity-driven growth allows the Fed to cut rates without inflation risk, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as growth is decoupled from inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 104.2062, PAYEMS at 159398 thousand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 60-day windows when productivity signals are strong, or yields fail to decline despite Fed rate cuts being implemented.
